📢 Company Update | YAAP Digital Appoints Sambit Mohanty as Group Chief Creative Officer

🔹 YAAP Digital (NSE: YAAP) has appointed Sambit Mohanty as Group Chief Creative Officer (GCCO), reinforcing its leadership team as the Company continues to scale its integrated marketing and digital capabilities.

🔹 A seasoned industry professional with over two decades of experience, Sambit joins YAAP from McCann Worldgroup India, where he served as Creative Head – North & South.

🔹 His career spans leadership roles across some of the industry's most respected networks, including JWT, DDB, Bates, Elephant Design and McCann Worldgroup.

🔹 At YAAP, he will spearhead creative strategy and excellence across the group, while strengthening the Company's offerings across creative, content, influencer marketing and AI-led marketing solutions.

🔹 His work has earned recognition at some of the world's most prestigious creative platforms, including Cannes Lions, Clio Awards, D&AD, One Show, Spikes Asia and Effies.

🔹 The appointment reflects YAAP's continued investment in high-calibre leadership talent and its commitment to building a world-class, technology-enabled marketing enterprise.

AFCOM Holdings Limited | Q4 FY26 Concall Highlights

Operational Highlights
• Dry lease operations generated Total Income of ₹528.71 Cr during FY26
• Handled 24,353.42 tonnes of cargo volume across 1,923 trips during FY26
• Completed 1,129 charter flights during FY26, highlighting strong demand across key cargo corridors
• Expanded operations across Sri Lanka, Maldives and the Middle East during FY26

Fleet Expansion
• Third aircraft operational
• Fourth and fifth aircraft expected to become operational before the next quarter
• Boeing 777 wide-body induction remains on track
• At least one Boeing 777 expected to be operational by the end of FY27

Strategic Updates
• Awarded Fastest Growing Airline in the Freighter Market by Aviation Cargo Express
• Entered the Australia-Pacific region through a strategic relationship with Nauru Air Corporation
• Received Designated Carrier Certification from the Government of India
• Successfully completed strategic capital raising for fleet expansion
• AFCOM to become the first cargo airline to operate from Noida International Airport's new cargo terminal

Key Financial Highlights
• Q4 FY26 Total Income stood at ₹191.88 Cr, EBITDA stood at ₹74.08 Cr and PAT stood at ₹44.66 Cr, reflecting YoY growth of 87.80%, 51.52% and 72.85%, respectively.
• FY26 Total Income stood at ₹587.72 Cr, EBITDA stood at ₹238.14 Cr and PAT stood at ₹121.90 Cr, reflecting YoY growth of 143.86%, 211.72% and 230.05%, respectively.

Growth Outlook
• Management expects strong growth supported by fleet expansion and robust cargo demand
• Additional aircraft capacity expected to be fully utilized
• Fourth and fifth aircraft expected to drive the next phase of growth
• Boeing 777 induction expected to significantly enhance cargo carrying capacity and network reach

NUVAMA SAYS - Only 2 Vedanta stocks to be excluded today 

Update (Nifty Next 50 Rejig) - Vedanta Power & Vedanta Iron & Steel Exclusions are Today (Announcement)
 
* As per the index methodology, Vedanta Power Ltd. and Vedanta Iron & Steel Ltd. did not hit the lower price band on two consecutive trading days (June 16 and June 17, 2026). Accordingly, NSE Indices has decided to exclude both stocks from the relevant indices effective June 19, 2026 (post close of June 18, 2026). Thus adjustment will be today in last thirty minutes.
 
* It may be noted that even if either stock hits the lower price band on June 18, 2026, the exclusion will not be deferred further.
 
* The remaining two entities, Vedanta Aluminium and Vedanta Oil & Gas, are yet to be excluded from the Nifty Next 50. We expect these exclusions to take place over the next few days. We have also highlighted the potential passive outflows associated with both entities.

Afcom Cargo Makes Historic First Landing at Noida International Airport

• AFCOM Holdings Limited became the first standalone cargo airline to start operations at Noida International Airport, with its aircraft marking the first cargo flight.

• Operations are facilitated by AISATS, the cargo terminal operator at the airport.

• AFCOM signed an agreement with AISATS to operate cargo services, strengthening its network in North India.

• The start of operations positions AFCOM among early beneficiaries of the upcoming logistics and air cargo hub at Noida.

India Battery Energy Storage

Key Finding
- India lost 2.1 TWh renewable power in FY26
- Renewable energy curtailed to support coal operations
- Represents 1.3% of renewable generation
- Estimated value loss: ₹629 Cr

Why Curtailment Happened
- Coal plants operating at Minimum Technical Load (MTL)
- Coal units generally cannot reduce below ~55% capacity
- Excess solar generation forces renewable curtailment
- Structural grid flexibility challenge emerging

Storage Requirement
- India needs ~10 GWh battery storage immediately
- Storage can absorb excess daytime solar power
- Reduces renewable energy wastage
- Improves grid balancing capability

Solar Growth
- 24 GW solar capacity added between Oct 2025–Apr 2026
- Total installed solar capacity reaches ~154 GW
- Renewable growth outpacing grid flexibility
- Storage deployment lagging generation additions

Grid Stress Indicators
- Solar and wind reached 41% of midday generation
- Coal generation required massive ramping cycles
- Curtailment becoming a regular balancing tool
- Renewable down-regulation increasing rapidly

Worsening Trend
- Renewable curtailment exceeds 3,600 GWh by June 2026
- More than 1,400 GWh added since March 2026
- Curtailment volumes accelerating sharply
- No longer an isolated event

Who Benefits
Battery Storage
- Battery Energy Storage Systems (BESS)
- Grid-scale storage developers
- Battery manufacturers
- Energy management providers

Power Equipment
- BESS integrators
- Inverter manufacturers
- Transformer suppliers
- Grid modernization companies

Renewable Ecosystem
- Solar developers
- Hybrid renewable projects
- Round-the-clock renewable providers
- Dispatchable clean energy operators

Policy Implications
- Faster BESS tenders likely
- Storage-linked renewable projects may accelerate
- Grid flexibility becomes policy priority
- Energy security benefits increase

Investment Theme
- India is transitioning from renewable generation buildout
- Next phase focuses on storage and grid flexibility
- Batteries becoming critical infrastructure
- Storage likely to see multi-year investment cycle

Key Monitorables
- BESS auction pipeline
- Storage project commissioning
- Battery manufacturing capacity additions
- Renewable curtailment trends
- Grid modernization initiatives

Key Takeaway
- India's renewable challenge is no longer generation capacity but grid flexibility
- ₹629 Cr worth of renewable electricity was lost in FY26 because storage infrastructure has not kept pace with solar growth
- The need for 10 GWh of immediate battery storage highlights that BESS is rapidly becoming the next critical investment theme in India's energy transition

Regulatory Update
- CDSCO suspends KR Unit Ethics Committee
- Suspension period: 24 months
- Action linked to compliance deficiencies
- Related to clinical trial regulations

Scope of Restriction
- Cannot approve new clinical trials
- Cannot oversee BA/BE studies
- Limited to KR Unit Ethics Committee
- Does not extend to other HCG units

Operational Impact
- Hospital operations unaffected
- Patient care continues normally
- No impact on core healthcare services
- Business continuity maintained

Company Response
- Reviewing regulatory order
- Exploring legal remedies
- Engaging with authorities
- Assessing compliance requirements

Risk Assessment
- Impact confined to clinical research activities
- No immediate effect on hospital revenues
- Reputational and compliance aspect to monitor
- Future regulatory developments important

Key Monitorables
- Outcome of legal proceedings
- Regulatory resolution timeline
- Restoration of committee approval
- Impact on research programs

Key Takeaway
- The suspension is a regulatory setback for the KR Unit Ethics Committee but does not affect HCG's hospital operations or patient care activities
- Near-term business impact appears limited, though compliance and regulatory developments will remain important to track

FED OUTCOME – HAWKISH BEGINNINGS !

10 points.

1. Kevin Warsh’s first FOMC as Chair marked a clear hawkish shift.

2. Fed upgraded it’s inflation outlook (core PCE to 3.3%) & removed it’s easing bias

3. The dot plot skewed more restrictive, with 9 participants penciling in a 2026 hike & median no longer showing a 2026 cut

4. Of the 9 participants looking for a hike this year, three look for one hike, five for two hikes, one for three hikes. Only one participant is still looking for a cut

5. The post-meeting statement was trimmed down significantly

6. The forward guidance was dropped entirely, with the only thing close being a reference that: “The Committee will deliver price stability.”

7. Instead of answering any questions about the economy or monetary policy, Warsh announced the creation of five task forces to study various aspects of      monetary policy

8. In the press conference, Chair Warsh gave no forward guidance

9. Warsh mostly dwelled on new chair topics, including his five task forces

10. They will deal with: (1) communications (2) the balance sheet (3) data gathering (4) productivity & jobs (5) inflation drivers

US IRAN DEAL

President Donald Trump signed an interim deal to end the war with Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, speeding up the timeline for the agreement to go into effect despite blowback from Republicans who said it amounted to a victory for Tehran.

The so-called memorandum of understanding is now in effect, a US official said. It was unclear if Iran had immediately begun taking steps to fully reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Trump told reporters he signed the document at the palace of Versailles near Paris, where he had dinner with French President Emmanuel Macron. Versailles was where leaders of the great powers gathered in 1919 to sign the peace treaty that formally ended World War I.

Traders dumped short-term Treasuries and futures traders piled into bets on rate hikes after Federal Reserve chairman Kevin Warsh emphasized the central bank won’t tolerate high inflation.
The Fed’s message triggered repositioning in markets, with two-year Treasury yields shooting up and futures traders pricing in a quarter-point rate hike by October at around 60% probability .
Warsh underscored the Fed’s focus is shifting back to its mandate to combat inflation, but didn’t commit to raising rates and indicated he saw little reason to try to forecast such moves in advance.

The Fed Enters Its Warsh Era
New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh wrapped his first policy meeting today, holding interest rates steady and debuting a shortened policy statement and one less dot on the dot plot as he did not participate in the dot plot exercise.

It didn’t take Kevin Warsh long to get the bond market’s attention. Warsh forcefully drove home one point in his debut press conference as Federal Reserve chairman: the central bank won’t tolerate high inflation

In his first major announcement as Fed chair, Warsh detailed the formation of five task forces that will take on wide-ranging issues pertaining to various drivers and how they fit into monetary policy.

The committees will focus on communications, the Fed’s balance sheet, its reliance on data sources, productivity and jobs and the impact of artificial intelligence and other transformative technologies, and the central bank’s inflation “frameworks.

Warsh provided a rough outline on how he thinks the panels will operate and said more information will be provided in coming days.

“Each task force will serve an objective shared by everyone in the system, shared by everyone around that table that I sat with over the last couple of days, a Federal Reserve that is clear-eyed about its mission, fit for purpose, and focused on the future,” he said.

The Federal Open Market Committee voted unanimously Wednesday to hold its benchmark federal funds rate in a range of 3.5% to 3.75% in its first gathering under Warsh’s leadership.

Treasuries sold off, the dollar rallied and stocks fell after the decision was announced. Following Warsh’s press conference, traders were fully pricing in a rate hike by December with an even chance of an earlier hike by October.

Warsh's Fed started with a Hawkish Pause.

Bond yields shot up, US dollar strengthened and US stocks saw a >1% cut .
Focus was brought on price stability and controlling inflation

U.S. employers announced just over 97,000 job cuts in May 2026, according to a report released Thursday by outplacement firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas.

The announced cuts represent the highest May number since 2020, at the start of the Covid-19 pandemic, according to Challenger’s data. It’s also the third straight month that layoffs have risen this year, up from the 83,387 cuts reported in April, 60,620 in March and 48,307 in February.

According to the report, which is based on company announcements of job cuts, employers cited AI as the primary reason for almost 40% of May’s announced job cuts, up from 7% in January, 10% in February, 25% in March and 26% in April. The May number brings the total announced job cuts attributed to AI in the first five months of 2026 to 87,714, up from 54,836 throughout all of 2025.

Emerald Finance Limited collaborates with Bizvisory HR Global Solutions to deliver Early Wage Access.

Emerald Finance Limited has partnered with Bizvisory Hr Global Solutions Private Limited, Delhi to launch our new Early-Wage-Access (EWA) program.

This strategic initiative is designed to provide immediate financial flexibility  towards workforce through compensation access. Employees can draw down on their earned salary in real-time to cover day-to-day needs, with automated settlement during the regular monthly payroll.

Key Program Highlights:
* On-Demand Liquidity: Instant access to accrued earnings to match real-time cash demands.
* Sub-Cycle Financial Relief: Mitigates mid-month working capital pressures for employees.
* Automated Operational Flow: Features an integrated, tech-driven settlement process for streamlined, end-to-end payroll recovery.

This partnership accelerates Emerald Finance Limited’s expansion into high-yield, employer-linked salary advance solutions. It broadens retail financial services portfolio while equipping the workforce with a responsible, flexible liquidity tool.

Unihealth Hospitals Limited (NSE: UNIHEALTH) – Key Healthcare Milestone

Unihealth Hospitals Limited announced UMC Hospitals, Navi Mumbai completed its first IVUS-Guided Coronary Intervention.

The procedure introduces intravascular imaging technology, enabling precision-driven cardiac interventions with improved assessment and stent optimization.

This milestone strengthens UMC Hospitals’ cardiac platform and reflects focus on advanced medical infrastructure and technology-led healthcare delivery.

For FY26, Unihealth Hospitals reported Total Income ₹137.01 Cr, EBITDA ₹58.82 Cr and Net Profit ₹25.83 Cr.

💸 EMERALD FINANCE PARTNERS WITH BIZVISORY HR FOR EARLY WAGE ACCESS PROGRAM

• Emerald Finance has partnered with Bizvisory HR Global Solutions Pvt. Ltd. to offer its Early Wage Access (EWA) program to employees

• The EWA program allows employees to access a portion of their earned salary before the regular payday

• The company is expanding its salary advance solution as part of its retail lending growth strategy

• Under the arrangement, Emerald Finance will work with employers to provide short-term salary advance loans to employees

• Repayments will be recovered through salary deductions, helping reduce collection and credit risks

• The partnership strengthens Emerald Finance's presence in the embedded finance and employee financial wellness segment

• The initiative supports the company's strategy of expanding its financial services offerings and retail customer base

Role in Defence

- One of the most overlooked defence opportunities
- Revenue begins after platform delivery
- Long-duration maintenance contracts
- High recurring revenue potential

- Decades-long revenue streams
- Strong customer stickiness
- High entry and qualification barriers
- Lower cyclicality than new platform orders

- Growing installed defence base
- Aircraft and helicopter fleets
- Naval platforms
- Armoured vehicles
- Defence electronics upgrades

- Enhances fleet availability

Key Takeaway
- The defence opportunity does not end with platform delivery

- As India localizes maintenance and materials capabilities, this layer could become one of the most durable beneficiaries of the defence supercycle

India Defence Supercycle

Land Systems & Ammunition

Role in Defence
- Core of battlefield combat capability
- Supports ground-force effectiveness
- Critical for sustained military operations
- Backbone of army modernization

Key Segments
- Ammunition
- Artillery systems
- Armoured vehicles
- Land combat platforms
- Military logistics equipment

Key Insight
- Strongest visibility in defence demand
- Ammunition consumption higher than expected in modern conflicts
- Global stockpile rebuilding underway
- Long-duration procurement cycle emerging

Demand Drivers
- Defence modernization
- Strategic stockpiling
- Indigenous manufacturing push
- Export opportunities
- Rising geopolitical tensions

Industry Characteristics
- Recurring ammunition demand
- Capacity expansion opportunities
- Long-term contracts
- High utilization potential

Second-Order Effect
- Greater private-sector participation
- Expanding role in strategic supply chains
- Higher order book visibility
- Potential margin improvement through scale

Growth Opportunity
- Ammunition replenishment
- Artillery modernization
- Vehicle upgrades
- New defence procurement programs

Key Takeaway
- Land systems and ammunition offer some of the clearest demand visibility in defence
- Stockpile rebuilding can drive multi-year growth
- Private manufacturers are becoming increasingly important to India's defence ecosystem

reports.text:
India Defence Supercycle

Space & Defence Convergence

Role in Modern Warfare
- Space is becoming a strategic warfare layer
- Enables navigation, surveillance and communication
- Critical for intelligence gathering
- Supports precision-strike capabilities

Key Applications
- Satellites
- Earth observation systems
- Navigation technologies
- Military communications
- Space-based intelligence

Key Insight
- Limited listed space-focused companies
- Scarcity value for listed proxies
- Exposure through defence-space suppliers
- Growing strategic importance

Demand Drivers
- Satellite deployments
- Defence modernization
- Border surveillance needs
- Secure communication networks
- Space-based intelligence systems

Second-Order Effect
- Growth in AI-powered image analytics
- Demand for encrypted communications
- Data processing and interpretation opportunities
- Expansion of software-led defence capabilities

Value Chain
- Satellite manufacturing
- Optics and sensors
- Ground infrastructure
- Data analytics platforms
- Defence software integration

Strategic Importance
- Improves situational awareness
- Enhances military decision-making
- Supports network-centric warfare
- Strengthens national security infrastructure

Key Takeaway
- Space is evolving from a support function to a core defence capability
- Listed opportunities remain limited, creating scarcity value
- Long-term growth extends beyond satellites into AI, analytics and secure communications

India Defence Supercycle

Defence Software & AI

Role in Modern Warfare
- Decision-making layer of defence systems
- Enables faster intelligence processing
- Improves mission effectiveness
- Critical for network-centric warfare

Key Segments
- Defence software
- AI and machine learning
- Mission systems
- Command & control platforms
- Autonomous decision-support systems

Key Insight
- Most underappreciated defence segment
- High intellectual property content
- Asset-light business model
- Recurring revenue characteristics

Business Model Advantage
- Platforms sold once
- Software upgraded repeatedly
- Long-term maintenance contracts
- Continuous certification and mission updates

Demand Drivers
- Tejas and fighter programs
- Missile systems
- Naval platforms
- UAVs and autonomous systems
- Electronic warfare solutions

Second-Order Effect
- Every platform creates decades of software demand
- Higher software content per platform over time
- Growing need for AI-enabled capabilities
- Expanding lifecycle revenue opportunities

Strategic Importance
- Faster decision superiority
- Enhanced battlefield awareness
- Improved system interoperability
- Force multiplier across defence assets

Key Takeaway
- Defence software combines high margins with recurring revenue
- Software value grows throughout a platform's lifecycle
- AI and digital warfare are becoming critical pillars of future defence spending

India Defence Supercycle

Aerospace & UAVs

Role in Modern Warfare
- Air superiority and battlefield intelligence
- Critical for surveillance and reconnaissance
- Increasingly central to combat operations
- Force multiplier for armed forces

Key Segments
- UAVs and drones
- Loitering munitions
- Tactical strike systems
- Aerospace platforms
- Autonomous defence systems

Key Insight
- Fastest-growing defence segment
- Industry still largely unconsolidated
- No dominant private-sector leader yet
- Significant long-term market opportunity

Growth Drivers
- Defence modernization
- Indigenous drone development
- Export opportunities
- Increasing military adoption

Industry Evolution
- Shift from ISR to combat roles
- Integration of AI and autonomy
- Growing demand for anti-drone systems
- Rapid technology upgrades

Second-Order Effect
- Drones now part of first-strike strategy
- Procurement cycles shortening
- Faster deployment requirements
- Higher recurring demand for upgrades and replacements

Strategic Importance
- Lower-cost force projection
- Enhanced battlefield awareness
- Reduced operational risk
- Critical future warfare capability

Key Takeaway
- UAVs represent one of the largest growth opportunities in India's defence ecosystem
- Industry leadership is still evolving, creating room for new winners
- Rising deployment and faster procurement cycles support long-term demand growth

India Defence Supercycle

Missile & Precision Strike Systems

Role in Defence
- Core deterrence capability
- Enables precision targeting
- Critical for modern warfare
- Strengthens national security

Key Segments
- Missiles
- Ammunition
- Precision-guided weapons
- Rocket systems
- Strategic strike platforms

Business Models
- Large strategic missile programs
- Recurring ammunition demand
- Replenishment-driven orders
- Long-term defence contracts

Key Insight
- Missile systems generate large project orders
- Ammunition creates repeat demand
- Inventory replenishment drives recurring business
- Multiple revenue streams across the ecosystem

Demand Drivers
- Defence modernization
- Strategic stockpiling
- Export opportunities
- Rising geopolitical tensions

Second-Order Effect
- Conflicts deplete inventories rapidly
- Nations rebuilding stockpiles
- Sustained ammunition demand
- Multi-year order visibility for suppliers

Industry Characteristics
- High qualification barriers
- Critical national-security products
- Long procurement cycles
- Strong government support

Key Takeaway
- Missile and ammunition ecosystem is a key beneficiary of defence spending
- Strategic stockpiling creates recurring demand beyond new platform purchases
- Precision-strike capabilities remain a long-term growth area in defence

India Defence Supercycle

Defence Electronics & Sensors

Role in Modern Warfare
- Intelligence layer of defence systems
- Enables detection, tracking and targeting
- Critical for decision-making superiority
- Powers network-centric warfare

Key Components
- Radars
- Electronic Warfare (EW) systems
- Sensors
- Communication systems
- Mission-critical electronics

Why It Matters
- Among highest-margin defence segments
- Strong technology and qualification barriers
- Limited qualified suppliers
- High switching costs

Revenue Characteristics
- Long product life cycles
- Recurring upgrade opportunities
- Strong order visibility
- Sticky customer relationships

Demand Drivers
- Fighter aircraft programs
- Naval platforms
- Missile systems
- Drones and UAVs
- Air defence networks

Second-Order Effect
- Electronics demand grows with every platform
- Content value per platform increasing
- Order books can outpace platform growth
- Benefits from defence modernization

Key Takeaway
- Defence electronics is the intelligence backbone of modern warfare
- High entry barriers and sticky contracts create durable growth
- One of the most attractive segments in India's defence value chain

India Defence Supercycle

Platform Primes
- Sit at top of defence value chain
- Build and integrate complete platforms
- Manage complex supply chains
- Control program execution

Role in Ecosystem
- Coordinate thousands of suppliers
- Own platform development lifecycle
- Drive production and delivery schedules
- Act as system integrators

Why They Matter
- High entry barriers
- Large contract sizes
- Long execution cycles
- Strong revenue visibility

Revenue Characteristics
- Single platform win can provide 10–15 years visibility
- Long-term order books
- Multi-phase execution opportunities
- Recurring upgrade and maintenance revenues

Second-Order Benefits
- Fighter aircraft programs
- Submarine programs
- Armoured vehicle programs
- Missile and defence systems

Strategic Importance
- Defence exports catalyst
- Supports indigenization efforts
- Strengthens national security ecosystem
- Emerging geopolitical relevance

Key Takeaway
- Platform primes are the biggest beneficiaries of the defence capex cycle
- Their success creates opportunities across the entire supplier ecosystem
- Long-duration programs provide sustained growth visibility

BOFA Sec on Tata Mot CV
Buy, TP Rs 470
CV cycle debate continues but mkt share trends getting better. Gaining share in HCVs & SCVs on platform refresh & new launch
Margin focus over growth chase intact despite near term cost pressure: Pricing discipline, mix, cost control & non truck revs
Iveco upside on EU LCV recovery, sourcing/capex synergies & BS deleveraging. Risk reward favorable, Adj EV/EBITDA at 10x

BoFA Sec on Tata Mot PV
U-P, TP Rs 335
Investor queries into JLR analyst day focus on margin roadmap given structural drag from China, warranty, VME, tariff persist
Margin recovery hinges on self-help program targeting lower break-even points & £1.7bn cost savings; specifics to be watched
Recent updates from EU OEM not encouraging.
Execution on JLR new launches, EV strategy, cost-out program key amid tough macro

Jefferies on PB Fin
Buy, TP Rs 1950
PB has derated c.20% over last 6 months due to concerns around commission regulations and founder exit, despite largely unchanged consensus earnings
Est. price is already factoring a 10% commission cut.
Further, business model is unlikely to change with renewal profitability unchanged.
Risks from founder stake sale may be overdone as business management teams are well-entrenched
Trades at 53x FY27 EV/Adj. EBITDA, with EBITDA doubling over FY27-29e.

Jefferies on AU Small Fin BK
Buy, TP Rs 1250
Management meet takeaways
Reiterated strong growth outlook, led by distribution-led gains.
Deposit growth is steady, though higher funding costs may cap NIM expansion, while op. leverage aids profitability.
Asset quality is strong, & FY27 credit cost guidance was retained at 90bps of assets, aiding RoA of 1.8%.
Premium vals (3x FY27E adj P/BV) are justified by growth & universal license upside.

Jefferies on Navin Fluorine
Buy, TP Raised to Rs 8700
Management meet takeaways
Company shared a roadmap of improving technical capability and adding new growth areas.
Strong pipelines in CDMO and cooling products and participation in new molecules in spec chem anchor medium-term growth
Project 22% EPS Cagr over FY26-29E. Valuation is at long-term average.

Jefferies on Polycab
Buy, TP Rs 10920
Despite 25% rally YTD, Polycab stays a conviction pick for JEF, led by consistent market share gains (31% now; +12% in 5Y), strong order book in RDSS & Bharat Net, diverse sales, power proxy and FMEG sustaining +ive EBIT.
In past 15-Q, Polycab has posted double-digit C&W sales growth with 12-15% EBIT

JPM on Bajaj Hsg Fin
UW, TP Rs 70
Management meet key takeaways
Most important takeaway is that BT-out (attrition) trends have tapered down QTD, after staying elevated over last 9-12 months
This offers a positive read-through for entire HFC sector
That said, select private banks have turned more aggressive in prime-mortgage segment, with PSBs (ex-SBI) also keeping competition elevated (
On BFL’s 86.7% stake in BHFL, BFL management has guided for a significant stake sell down at right price, rather than piecemeal (regulatory obligation to lower stake to 75% by Sep-29)
FY27 guidance will be announced post 1Q print (expected 29th July), with medium-term RoA (2-2.2%) and RoE (13-15%) guidance unchanged.

JPMorgan on Bajaj Housing Finance
Maintain Underweight with TP of Rs 70
Attrition tapering offers sector tailwind
Focus on high-yield segments
Medium-term RoA/RoE guidance intact
The franchise is best-in-class
But expensive valuations limit stock upside

CLSA on LG Electronic
Initiate O-P, TP Rs 1830
Positioning it as a key beneficiary of India’s durable consumption upcycle
Expect large appliances segment to deliver double-digit CAGR, driven by rising penetration and a shift towards premiumisation, while LGEL’s strong brand, broad product portfolio and deep distribution reach should sustain leadership across key categories.
Operating leverage, coupled with an increasing backward integration, is likely to support steady margin expansion

reports.text:
HSBC on Cement
Cost inflation worries to abate, pricing requires industry discipline; outlook positive
Costs to peak in Q1, and decline in H2
Lower crude could drive costs down in FY28
While pause in new capacity adds is positive, industry discipline is key to cement price increases and earnings upgrades
Top picks: Ambuja, UltraTech, Dalmia, Nuvoco all Buys; retain Hold on JK Cement, Shree Cement

UBS on Internet Cos
Indian Internet stocks have had a difficult 2026 with stocks down 10-30% YTD on concerns surrounding weak macro, rising competition and potential disruption from AI.
Checks suggest GMV / NOV / billings growth across segments is stabilising after a slowdown in H2FY26, & competition in QC is also showing signs of moderation
Prefer Eternal - Buy; TP Rs305 & Meesho – Buy – TP Rs 210)
Upgrade Info Edge to Buy- TP Rs 1280 following stock's 25% YTD correction
Nykaa: Widening competitive moat, but valuations a key bottleneck; Neutral, TP Rs 290
Swiggy – Buy, TP Rs 335

Jefferies on PB Fintech
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 1950
PB has derated 20% over last 6 months due to concerns around commission regulations and founder exit
Est. the price is already factoring a 10% commission cut
Further, the business model is unlikely to change with renewal profitability unchanged
Risks from founder stake sale may be overdone as business management teams are well-entrenched

Citi on Colgate
Maintain Sell with TP of Rs 2050
Growth trajectory should be more balanced; a favorable base is helping
Pricing is returning, but net realization may remain moderated by competitive intensity
Premiumization remains the key structural growth lever
Margin trade-offs are likely to persist despite healthy gross margins

JPM on Dixon
OW, TP Rs 12700
Revisiting Vivo JV approval scenario
Co had highlighted that Vivo has 35mn annual volumes in India of which 67% will come to Dixon, so 22mn.
As per management, JV could lead to a revenue uplift of Rs300bn with a higher average selling price compared to the existing mobile portfolio.
If JV is approved in June then would expect operations to start after 60-90 days in 3QFY27
Assume it could contribute 11mn to mobile volumes in FY27 and 22mn in FY28.
This could drive a 24-39% upgrade to revenue estimates over FY27-28E but a lower 13-18% EPS upgrade due to 51:49 JV format that drives minority interest.

Citi on Trent
Maintain Sell with TP of Rs 2733
Demand appears to be holding up reasonably well
Supply-side remain noisy across RM (polyester/cotton), labour availability, etc
inflation impact in the medium-term is limited
Prefer not to pass on the entire cost impact
Real estate remains an important variable, but Trent’s asymmetric lease structures continue to provide a strong option value
Zudio’s opportunity appears larger than earlier envisaged
Trent continues to evaluate lifestyle adjacencies

UBS on Coforge
Neutral, TP Rs 1505
Investor day highlights
Management highlighted non-linear growth in enterprise AI spend and its impact on services demand.
Management expects post AI outsourced IT spend to grow 40-50% over next 3-5 years, a key tailwind for Coforge's growth.
Company stated
1) scaling up key accounts;
2) scaling up big bets;
3) partner-led growth;
4) acquisitions to drive growth.
Revenue of US$2.5bn in FY26 is likely to grow to US$5bn by FY30 (CAGR of 19%) including inorganic.
Within this, management expects verticals like healthcare & hitech and government outside India to grow 17-18% followed by insurance and travel at 14% and BFSI at 12%.
Margin expansion led by operational discipline; capital allocation to prioritize growth

Jefferies on Coforge
Buy, TP Rs 1860
Coforge highlighted its US$5bn revenue aspiration (19% CAGR; 15% organic), led by account mining, AI-led services and ecosystem partnerships, with selective M&A support.
Coforge is pivoting to an AI-native, platform-driven model, combining domain expertise with reusable assets
Move to FDE-led, mod squad delivery is improving productivity and enabling larger, outcome-based deals, supporting sustained profitable growth.

Nomura on Coforge
Buy, TP Rs 2100
Investor day takeaways
Eyeing $5bn of revenue by FY30E with significant tailwinds from AI
Disciplined financial rigour to continue
AI is central to delivery org
Solution team aiding in large deal win momentum
Coforge continues to work on its playbook of scaling accounts, scaling its big bets (focus areas), partner-led growth & acquisitions to double its revenue by FY30

HSBC on Coforge
Buy, TP Rs 1710
Investor day takeaways
Coforge believes AI to be a net tailwind for its business and has set FY30e revenue target at USD5bn; inclusive of a large M&A
Management is committed to FY27e EBIT margin of 16.5% and FCF/PAT of +100% with growth picking up from 2QFY27
Coforge retains growth leadership in tech services & now with better margin/FCF outlook

Citi on Crompton Consumer
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 400
Demand has started to improve, helped by strong summer and partly led by low base
Crompton’s products are relatively non-discretionary, but price shock can defer purchases and channel elasticity can shift share across brands
Crompton has taken 4 rounds of price hikes in fans aggregating 9–10%
Near term priority is revenue growth, while margin recovery should be more visible through FY27

Jefferies on Navin Fluorine
Maintain Buy; Hike TP to Rs 8700 from Rs 8385
Building Long-Term Growth
CDMO - US$ 200mn Revenue aspiration for 2030
Specialty Chem - Deepening and broadening with key clients
Cooling solutions - Multiple growth engines
Incubating the semiconductor chemicals vertical
Project 22% EPS Cagr over FY26-29; Valuation is at long-term average

Jefferies on AU SFB
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 1250
AUM growth outlook remains strong
Steady deposit growth; hike in rates may cap NIM expansion
Operating leverage to aid profitability
Universal bank transition is a key milestone; capital raise contingent
Asset quality trends are holding-up well
Strong growth & profitability support premium valuations

CLSA on RIL
O-P, TP Rs 1800
AR analysis reveals YoY rise in FCF was driven by a sharp decline in Capex even as OPCF fell 3% YoY
Reading indicates Opex and interest cost capitalisation equated to a fourth of Reliance’s consolidated core PBT, which may reduce operating leverage over coming years
Ambitious plans on AI offerings & datacentres, expansion of media & consumer businesses plus ramp-up of new energy value chains are strategic areas highlighted in its annual report
Further clarity on these along with value-unlocking plans will be watched for at its upcoming AGM.

**Jefferies on Polycab
Maintain Buy; Hike TP to Rs 10920 from Rs 9770**

Polycab stays a conviction pick, led by consistent market share gains
See strong order book in RDSS & Bharat Net, diverse sales, power proxy and FMEG sustaining positive EBIT
In past 15-quarters, Polycab has posted double-digit C&W sales growth with 12-15% EBIT
Est FY26-29 EPS CAGR at +22%

**JPMorgan on Dixon Tech
Maintain Overweight with TP of Rs 12700**

If the Vivo JV is approved in June then we expect the operations to start after 60-90 days in Q3FY27
Assume it could contribute 11 mn to mobile volumes in FY27 and 22 mn in FY28
This could drive a 24-39% upgrade to revenue estimates over FY27-28 and a 13-18% EPS upgrade
This could take our fair value to Rs 15,100 (23% potential upside)
Dixon remains top pick
See two potential positive catalysts in the short term - Vivo JV approval and announcement of a PLI 2.0 scheme

MS on Pine Labs
Maintain Overweight with TP of Rs 186
Outlined comprehensive strategy to drive sustainable growth of 20%+ while improving margins
Think there is room for in-store payment business to surprise positively over coming quarters
Expect a 15-20% revenue CAGR in-store payments medium term
Flow business accounts for 25-27% of revenues – aims to take it to 50%
Ruled out any material impact from any potential change in regulation related to breakage income

MS on Coforge
Maintain Overweight with TP of Rs 1500

Aspiration to reach $5bn in revenue by FY30 (including inorganic), which implies a CAGR of 15% on an organic basis
Preference of debt repayment over dividends can create shareholder value but needs to be evaluated by the Board
Liked the confidence showcased by management in its FY30 vision
Expectations for broad-based growth across verticals
Retain constructive view on Coforge as we expect it to deliver the strongest revenue CAGR over FY26-28
Believe in the current weak sector environment management's expectations may require even stronger execution than showcased in the past

UBS on Coforge
Maintain Neutral with TP of Rs 1505

Ambitious targets laid out for FY30
Recent growth trajectory to continue
Margin expansion led by operational discipline; capital allocation to prioritize growth
Large-deal scaling and account concentration driving growth
Pivot towards platformisation and hybrid talent models

Nomura on Coforge
Maintain Buy with TP of Rs 2100

Eyeing doubling of revenue by FY30E led by AI
Disciplined financial rigour to continue
AI is central to delivery organisation
Solution team aiding in large deal win momentum

**UBS on Pine Labs
Buy, TP Rs 220**

Investor day takeaways
On its core subscription biz, co is moving toward acquiring medium-sized enterprises & expanding its PoS offering across government sectors & OMCs
On affordability, co's GMV was Rs740bn in FY26 (+22% YoY), & it sees a long runway of strong growth.
EMI on UPI is also being expanded, which will help co retain profit pools as transactions continue to shift to UPI, as per them.
In issuing & acquiring, Pine Labs highlighted its longstanding merchant relationships and superior tech stacks as key differentiation points.
It also showcased AI transaction rails in preparation for likely disruptions.
Additionally, co maintained guidance for revenue growth of 20% + and margin expansion.
Estimate a 19% revenue CAGR in FY26-28.

CITI On Pine Labs
Buy, TP Rs 235
Management meet takeaways
Growth guidance of 21-23% YoY
B2B Fintech is seeing improving monetization environment from a competitive perspective and Pine Labs has opportunities to increase attach rates across its merchant, bank & brand partnerships
Expect 18.5% top-line CAGR over FY26-28E, Adj EBITDA margins to expand to 26%/30% in FY27/28E vs 21% in FY26
Emkay Global on Metals and Mining Sector
Morgan Stanley maintains 'Overweight' ratings on Pine Labs with target price of Rs 186.
Motilal Oswal on JSW Steel Growth Outlook
**We will updates Regularly stay here**

reports.text:
SONA COMSTAR : APPROVES ₹62.6 CR CAPEX FOR ADVANCED ROBOTICS BUSINESS 🤖⚙️

• 🚀 Sona Comstar has approved a capital expenditure (Capex) of ₹62.6 crore (₹626 million) to enter the robotics industry, including advanced robotics. 

• 🤖 The new business line will focus on the manufacturing of components and systems for advanced robotics, marking a strategic diversification beyond the automotive sector. 

• 🌐 The initiative is expected to provide access to new end markets and application areas outside the automotive industry. 

• 📈 The company expects the move to broaden its product portfolio and create long-term growth opportunities. 

• 🏭 The investment was approved by the Board based on the recommendation of the Capex Committee. 

• 👨‍💼 The Board also appointed Mr. Pankaj Gupta as Senior Vice President (Legal), Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, effective June 17, 2026. 

• 🔄 Ms. Suman Poddar and Mr. Arjun Singh resigned from their interim roles as Company Secretary and Compliance Officer, respectively, following the appointment of a permanent appointee.  

🟢 Impact: Positive (Sona Comstar’s entry into advanced robotics diversifies its business beyond automotive, opens new growth avenues and strengthens its positioning in emerging technologies.)

reports.text:
SENCO GOLD : ICRA UPGRADES CREDIT RATING TO A+ / A1 ⭐💎

• 📈 ICRA has upgraded Senco Gold’s credit ratings by one notch, reflecting improved business performance and credit profile. 

• 🏦 The company’s ₹2,400 crore working capital facilities have been upgraded to [ICRA] A+ (Stable) / A1 from [ICRA] A (Stable) / A2+. 

• 💰 The ₹495 crore Fixed Deposit Programme has also been upgraded to [ICRA] A+ (Stable) from [ICRA] A (Stable).

• 🚔 Inventure Growth & Securities has filed a criminal complaint with the MIDC Police Station, Mumbai, with a copy marked to the Economic Offences Wing (EOW) regarding fraudulent misuse of its name and identity. 

• 🕵️ Unknown third parties allegedly cloned the company’s name and goodwill to illegally solicit and misappropriate public investment monies. 

• 🏦 The accused reportedly operated under the deceptively similar name “Inventure Growth and Securities” (without “Limited”) and used a bank account to collect funds from investors. 

• 📄 The perpetrators are alleged to have used counterfeit documents bearing the company’s name to falsely claim association and deceive retail clients. 

• 💰 The total amount of public money collected is currently unknown and remains subject to investigation by police authorities. 

• 🛡️ The company clarified that none of its assets, liquidity or bank accounts have been impacted, with exposure limited to brand protection risks. 

• 🚫 Inventure has categorically stated that it has no association whatsoever with the accused individuals or entities and is cooperating fully with authorities to freeze accounts and trace funds.

📢 Company Update | Repono Limited

Repono Secures Landmark 20-Year Contract from Reliance Industries Limited

Key Highlights:

🔹 Repono Limited has signed a 20-year multi-crore agreement with Reliance Industries Limited to develop a greenfield POL terminal for petrol, Diesel & Ethanol storage in western Uttar Pradesh.

🔹 The Company will undertake the entire project lifecycle, including Engineering, Procurement & Construction (EPC) followed by long-term Operations & Maintenance (O&M) of the terminal.

🔹 The project will be executed through Repono Mathura Terminal Private Limited, the Company's dedicated SPV.

🔹 The proposed facility will be a modern rail-fed terminal designed for automated bulk receipt, storage, handling and dispatch of Petrol, Diesel and Ethanol.

🔹 The terminal will operate as an open-access multi-user facility, catering to Reliance Industries while also providing capacity to other private and PSU Oil Marketing Companies (OMCs).

🔹 Once operational, the facility is expected to become a key fuel logistics hub supporting the growing automotive and industrial ecosystem across Western Uttar Pradesh.

🔹 Construction activities are set to commence immediately, with commercial operations targeted within 36 months.

🔹 The project combines Repono's EPC execution capabilities with its long-standing O&M expertise, further strengthening its position in India's petroleum and petrochemical infrastructure sector.

Unihealth Hospitals Limited Announces Strategic Share Swap to Increase Stake in Victoria Hospital Limited, Uganda

Unihealth Hospitals Limited (NSE: UNIHEALTH) has announced a strategic share-swap transaction to increase its ownership stake in Victoria Hospital Limited, Uganda, further strengthening its international healthcare platform and enhancing long-term shareholder value.

Key Transaction Highlights:

• Unihealth currently holds 50% equity ownership in Victoria Hospital Limited and proposes to acquire additional equity from existing shareholders through a share-swap arrangement, subject to applicable approvals and customary closing conditions.

• The transaction will enable Unihealth to increase strategic control and participate more significantly in the future growth of Victoria Hospital, a key healthcare asset in Uganda.

Strategic & Financial Benefits for Unihealth Shareholders:

✅ Enhanced Earnings Potential: Greater ownership participation in a profitable and growing healthcare asset is expected to improve earnings visibility and enhance consolidated profitability attributable to Unihealth shareholders.

✅ Value Creation Through Ownership Consolidation: The increased stake strengthens alignment of strategic objectives, enables deeper operational integration and provides greater participation in Victoria Hospital’s future expansion opportunities.

✅ Capital Efficient Growth: The share-swap structure allows Unihealth to expand its international healthcare footprint without significant immediate cash outflow, preserving liquidity for further growth initiatives.

✅ Strong Underlying Asset Performance: Victoria Hospital delivered robust performance in FY 2025-26, with revenue of ₹114.47 crore compared to ₹85.99 crore in the previous year and profit after tax of ₹43.63 crore compared to ₹25.81 crore, reflecting strong operational execution and profitability.

Victoria Hospital operates the 120-bedded UMC Victoria Hospital in Kampala, and the 30-bedded UMC Hospital in Entebbe, Uganda. The hospitals are tertiary care facilities, offering advanced clinical services across multiple specialties including orthopaedics, spine, nephrology, oncology, cardiology, gasteroenterology, urology, general and laparoscopic surgery, dialysis, diagnostics, IVF, paediatrics and critical care.

The transaction represents another important step in Unihealth’s strategy of building a scalable healthcare platform across emerging markets, leveraging established hospital infrastructure, clinical expertise and regional partnerships to drive sustainable growth.

Unihealth remains focused on creating long-term value for shareholders through disciplined expansion, operational excellence and strategic growth initiatives.

• 🇨🇳 The investigation covered imports of Thermoplastic Polyurethane (TPU)-based surface/Paint Protection Film from the People’s Republic of China. 

• ⏳ The final notification from the Central Government is still awaited, after which the duty may become effective. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Anti-dumping duty can curb low-cost Chinese imports, improve pricing power and support market share gains for Garware Hi-Tech Films.)

PINE LABS : DENIES REPORT ON GIFT CARD INCOME IMPACT 🚫💳

• 📰 Pine Labs has termed the media report titled “Inside Pine Labs’ profit story: The gift card income stream set to take a hit” as speculative, incorrect and misleading. 

• 🤝 The company clarified that its regulated products largely operate through co-branded program structures, where any breakage income belongs to partner brands, not Pine Labs. 

• 💰 Pine Labs stated that breakage income has never been a material part of its revenue or profit pool. 

• 📊 The company emphasized that such income has never been recognized in its P&L, and hence does not contribute meaningfully to earnings. 

• 🏦 Even if the RBI issues fresh regulations regarding breakage income, Pine Labs does not foresee any meaningful impact on its business, revenue or profitability. 

• ⏳ The co-branded operating model has been in place for over a decade across Pine Labs’ regulated programs in India. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The clarification removes concerns over potential regulatory risks and reassures investors that any RBI action is unlikely to materially impact Pine Labs’ financials.)

• ⚠️ TT Limited announced the lapse of 8,00,000 convertible warrants issued on a preferential basis after holders failed to exercise them within the stipulated period. 

• 💸 The company has forfeited ₹2.44 crore, representing the 25% upfront subscription amount paid at the time of allotment. 

• 📅 The warrants, allotted on December 16, 2024, had an 18-month tenure ending on June 15, 2026. 

• 🚫 Warrant holders failed to pay the remaining 75% consideration, leading to the lapse of all warrants. 

• 👥 VASM Consultants Pvt Ltd (2 lakh warrants) and Subhash Phootarmal Rathod (6 lakh warrants) were the affected warrant holders. 

• 📊 The company clarified that there will be no change in paid-up share capital, while the forfeited amount will be retained in accordance with accounting standards.

IndianCLSA ON DIXON 
DOWNGRADE TO UNDERPERFORM , TARGET PRICE 10,400

Recent rally on expected approvals seen in shares of Dixon Technologies is "overdone." 

Shares of Dixon Tech have rallied 24% from the March 30 closing levels of ₹9,637. Since then, the stock has gained 15% in April, and 3% each in May and June so far. 

Brokerage expects Dixon Tech's organic volumes to decline in financial year 2027 as it expects elevated memory prices to have a disproportionate impact on the Indian smartphone volumes.

Further, a delay in backward integration projects could also pose downside risks to estimates for the stock, according to CLSA, who added that with domestic smartphone market share plateauing, it expects growth to decelerate over the medium-term.

At 46 times financial year 2028 estimated price-to-earnings, CLSA finds Dixon's risk-reward to be unfavourable

SUVEN LIFE SCIENCES COMPLETES PHASE-1 STUDY OF CNS DRUG SUVN-16107 🧠💊

• ✅ Successfully completed First-in-Human (FIH) Phase-1 clinical study of SUVN-16107, a Muscarinic M1 Positive Allosteric Modulator (M1-PAM)

• 🚀 The drug candidate has now advanced into Phase-2 clinical development

• 🛡️ Study demonstrated a favourable safety profile with no dose-limiting toxicities or serious adverse events

• 🎯 Achieved projected therapeutic exposure in plasma and at the target site

• 🧬 Biomarker assessments showed evidence of central nervous system (CNS) activity and enhanced brain function

• 🧑‍⚕️ Pharmacokinetic assessments found no clinically meaningful differences between male and female participants

• 🍽️ Food intake had no clinically relevant impact on the drug's pharmacokinetics, supporting flexible dosing

• 🔬 SUVN-16107 is the fifth internally discovered NCE by Suven to enter clinical development, strengthening its CNS pipeline

• 📚 Detailed study findings are expected to be presented at medical conferences and peer-reviewed journals

🟢 Impact: Positive (Successful Phase-1 completion and progression to Phase-2 de-risks the asset and enhances the value of Suven's CNS pipeline.)

reports.text:
*JPM on L&T*
OW, TP Raised to Rs 5,060 from Rs4,570
L&T has demonstrated solid delivery under strategic plan Lakshya -26 (2021-2026).
Under plan, L&T:
1) has shown a 20% CAGR in its order inflows vs. target of 14%;
2) revenue at a 16% CAGR vs. target of 15%;
3) consol. ROE of 16.6% in FY26, up from 10% in FY21 (target of 18%).
L&T enters current 5-year strategic plan (Lakshya -31 over 2026-2031) building on this success - aiming to deliver:
1) a 10%-12% CAGR in order inflow; and,
2) a revenue CAGR of 12-15% over FY26-FY31 with 16%-17% consolidated ROE.
With re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz, tail risk to FY27 guidance has been removed
FY27 guidance given by L&T is healthy with 10-12% YoY growth in OI and Revenue and flat margins.
In addition, L&T enjoys optionality from: 1) an increase in capex in oil and gas in ME (e.g. UAE exiting OPEC, additional pipelines and storage infra, etc.); and, 2) acceleration in core infrastructure capex as re-construction opportunities in the Middle East materialize

*CITI on Tata Cons*
Buy, TP Rs 1450
Management meet Key takeaways
(a) Demand remains resilient, with no visible moderation so far.
(b) Growth outlook remains strong, led by sustained momentum in growth businesses (Sampann, RTD/NourishCo, Organic India/Capital Foods).
(c) Commodity environment largely benign near-term (tea, coffee).
(d) Pricing actions remain calibrated, with no broad-based hikes.
(e) Margin expansion trajectory remains intact (50–80bps improvement in FY27) despite near-term pressures, supported by mix, cost savings and operating leverage; ambition to reach 17%+ EBTIDA margin over the long-term.
Overall, management remains confident in sustaining strong growth momentum (continued scaling of the growth portfolio, robust innovation, and expanding distribution reach).
While near-term cost pressures persist, structural drivers for margin expansion remain intact

*CITI on Polycab*
Buy, TP Rs 10500
Management meet Key takeaways
(a) domestic demand has recovered after a weak March, which was impacted by geopolitical developments in West Asia; management saw healthy secondary sales from early April, with channel inventory at normalized levels;
(b) April/May saw mid- to high-single digit volume growth despite a high base (25%+ volume growth in 1QFY25);
(c) took another ~8–10% price hikes in 1Q to pass on the full imp act of higher commodity prices and currency depreciation;
(d) exports should grow faster than domestic in FY27, aided by US restart, higher enquiries and new geographies;
(e) EHV remains meaningful optionality, with ~Rs8bn capex broadly completed and potential revenue of ~Rs24bn at ~3x asset turn;
(f) C&W margins should remain near the higher end of 12–14%;
(g) BharatNet and RDSS combined will likely generate ~Rs18-20bn in revenue in FY27.

*CITI on RR Cable*
Buy, TP Rs 2650
Management meet Key takeaways
(a) demand continues to remain resilient – while export witnessed some disruption in March, April overall (domestic and export) was very strong, and management remains confident of double-digit volume growth in 1Q and FY27;
(b) have taken cumulative 25-30%+ price hike (in last 12 months) across Cables & Wires to pass on the full impact of higher commodity and currency depreciation;
(c) exports have rebounded sharply over the past 3–4 weeks, with management expecting both growth and margins to improve, aided by operating leverage;
(d) competition from new entrant should be manageable as wires/cables differ materially from paints — industry margins are lower, channel overlap is negligible with building material, approvals remain key for cables, and the market remains fragmented
(e) FMEG to breakeven in FY27, helped by strong traction in fans and pricing actions.

*Citi on Nykaa*
Recommendation Sell, Target ₹225
BPC growth momentum driven by growth in premium segments

reports.text:
*Nomura on Oil & Gas*
Expect oil marketing companies, city gas distributors &  PLNG to benefit most from potential opening of the Strait of Hormuz
(SoH).
Upstream companies ONGC/Oil India  will likely face maximum negative impact due to lower realization on both oil and gas
Impact on GAIL will likely be moderately positive as upside from increased transmission volume will likely be partly offset due to lower
marketing margins & reduced margins on LPG production as the import parity price of LPG could come down.
Lastly, expect a moderate negative impact on Reliance due to lower refining GRMs as ~3mbpd of Middle East refining capacity may come back online.
Believe this will be partly offset by the higher volume of off-gas available for co’s petchem plants (as government may relax the gas pooling mechanism).

Peace in the Middle East should gradually normalize energy prices.
Marketing losses on petrol and diesel at Spot Brent have narrowed to Rs (-) 2/lt and Rs (-) 11/lt.
Assuming normative GRMs, marketing margins at Spot Brent will rise above normative levels.
This may lead to some increase in excise duties that were lowered in March.

reports.text:
TCS TO TAKE ADDITIONAL $70 MN ( ₹ 662 Cr ) CHARGE IN DXC LAWSUIT ⚖️💻

• 🇺🇸 The U.S. Supreme Court has denied TCS' petition for review in the lawsuit filed by Computer Sciences Corporation (DXC Technology)

• 💰 TCS had already provided US$150 million for the matter in its books

• 📉 The company will now make an additional provision of US$70 million towards damages, interest and legal costs

• 📆 The additional charge will be recognized as a one-time exceptional expense in Q1 FY27

• ⚖️ The case pertains to litigation involving DXC Technology and follows prior disclosures made by TCS

• 🧾 The provision is expected to be accounted for in accordance with applicable accounting standards

🔴 Impact: Negative (The additional US$70 million provision will weigh on Q1 FY27 earnings, though it helps bring greater clarity and closure to the long-running legal dispute.)

Nomura on Oil & Gas

Expect oil marketing companies, city gas distributors &  PLNG to benefit most from potential opening of the Strait of Hormuz
(SoH).

Upstream companies ONGC/Oil India  will likely face maximum negative impact due to lower realization on both oil and gas

Impact on GAIL will likely be moderately positive as upside from increased transmission volume will likely be partly offset due to lower

marketing margins & reduced margins on LPG production as the import parity price of LPG could come down.

Lastly, expect a moderate negative impact on Reliance due to lower refining GRMs as ~3mbpd of Middle East refining capacity may come back online.

Believe this will be partly offset by the higher volume of off-gas available for co’s petchem plants (as government may relax the gas pooling mechanism).

ARVIND SMARTSPACES ADDS ₹180 CR PROJECT IN SOUTH AHMEDABAD 🏡📍

• 🏗️ Arvind SmartSpaces has signed a new residential horizontal development project in South Ahmedabad

• 📐 The project spans 58.25 acres with a saleable area of 2.5 million sq. ft.

• 💰 The project carries an estimated top-line potential of ₹180 crore

• 🤝 The development is structured as a Joint Development Agreement (JDA)

• 🌆 Located on Kerala-Nalsarovar Road, the project targets the fast-growing plotted development and weekend homes segment

• 📈 South Ahmedabad is emerging as a promising micro-market, supported by improving infrastructure and rising demand for plotted developments

• 🏘️ The project strengthens Arvind SmartSpaces' presence in the horizontal real estate segment and expands its Ahmedabad portfolio

🟢 Impact: Positive (The project enhances future revenue visibility, expands the land bank through an asset-light model, and reinforces the company's growth pipeline.)

Adani Enterprises Ltd. & Jabil Inc. are targeting a strategic alliance to establish a world-class, vertically integrated AI & data center infrastructure manufacturing platform in India 🇮🇳.

This collaboration aims to leverage Jabil's extensive engineering & manufacturing expertise with Adani Group's vast infrastructure, green energy, & logistics network, along with its growing domestic data center operations. The partnership is set to address the surging demand for AI-ready data center hardware globally.

Key Pillars of the Manufacturing Platform:

- 𝗚𝗶𝗴𝗮-𝗦𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗲 𝗔𝗜 𝗥𝗮𝗰𝗸 𝗔𝗿𝗰𝗵𝗶𝘁𝗲𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲: Plans to deploy multi-GW capacity for high-density AI Rack manufacturing in India, catering to global hyperscalers, co-location facilities, & enterprise data centers. This includes advanced manufacturing of next-gen liquid-cooled AI racks, servers, storage, & networking systems.
- 360-𝗗𝗲𝗴𝗿𝗲𝗲 𝗔𝗜 𝗜𝗻𝗳𝗿𝗮𝘀𝘁𝗿𝘂𝗰𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗘𝗰𝗼𝘀𝘆𝘀𝘁𝗲𝗺: Encompasses manufacturing of white space & grey space devices such as Power Distribution Units (PDUs), Coolant Distribution Units (CDUs), Transformers, Switchgears, Bus Bars, & advanced thermal management systems. The goal is to offer an end-to-end, design-to-deployment hardware ecosystem.
- 𝗠𝗮𝗿𝗸𝗲𝘁 𝗦𝗰𝗮𝗹𝗲 & 𝗧𝗿𝗮𝗻𝘀𝗶𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻 𝘁𝗼 𝗙𝗶𝗻𝗮𝗹 𝗗𝗼𝗰𝘂𝗺𝗲𝗻𝘁𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻: This initiative targets a global market opportunity exceeding USD 3 trillion over the next 7 years, driven by AI compute investments. Definitive operational frameworks & formal documentation are currently being developed.

𝗦𝘁𝗿𝗮𝘁𝗲𝗴𝗶𝗰 𝗥𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹𝗲:

- India's data center market is projected to reach 5-8 GW by 2030, driven by AI demand & data localization. 📈
- Global hyperscalers are investing over USD 50 billion in India's digital infrastructure.
- The alliance supports India's 'Make in India' initiative, aiming to transform the nation into a dominant global manufacturer & exporter of AI hardware.
- Aligns with Adani Group's commitment to develop 5 GW of green-energy-powered data centers by 2035.
- Jabil, with USD 29.8 billion revenue in FY25, brings significant expertise in power management & thermal solutions.

Mr. Gautam Adani, Chairman, Adani Group, stated, "Our alliance with Jabil represents a decisive step in building India's complete AI infrastructure stack — from green power generation to world-class hardware manufacturing. Together, we will ensure India is not merely a consumer in the AI age, but a creator, builder, and exporter of intelligence." 🚀

Mr. Mike Dastoor, CEO, Jabil, added, "By combining Jabil's more than sixty years of engineering expertise & advanced manufacturing capabilities with Adani's formidable infrastructure & energy platform, we can expect to execute down to the rack level for hyperscalers & enterprises here in India & across the globe."

ASHOK LEYLAND & SWITCH MOBILITY BECOME FIRST OEMs UNDER DELHI-NCR VEHICLE SCRAPPAGE SCHEME 🚛⚡

• 🤝 The Ministry of Road Transport & Highways (MoRTH) has signed its first MoU under the Delhi-NCR vehicle replacement scheme with Ashok Leyland and its EV arm Switch Mobility

• 🏷️ The companies will offer an 8% discount on ex-showroom prices of eligible trucks and buses under the scheme

• ⚡ For electric vehicles, the discount will be capped at the equivalent ICE vehicle discount in the same GVW category

• 💰 In addition to OEM discounts, the Central Government will provide a 5% interest subvention and monthly fuel vouchers for 5 years

• 🏛️ Participating State Governments will offer up to 100% motor vehicle tax concession for 10 years and waiver of registration fees

• 🌱 The scheme targets replacement of BS-IV and older vehicles in Delhi-NCR with BS-VI or electric vehicles, aiming to reduce pollution and modernize fleets

• 🚚 More automobile OEMs are expected to join the scheme in the coming days

🟢 Impact: Positive (Ashok Leyland gains first-mover advantage in fleet replacement, while the scheme could boost truck and bus demand, including electric vehicles.)

RAILTEL : BAGS ₹15.78 CR ORDER FROM MUNITIONS INDIA 🚆📡

• 🏭 RailTel has received a ₹15.78 crore work order from Munitions India Limited. 

• 📶 The project involves upgradation of MPLS bandwidth for the existing COMNET 2.0 network across 45 units of 7 DPSUs and DoO, New Delhi. 

• 🇮🇳 The order has been awarded by a domestic entity and is classified as a work order. 

• ⏳ The project is scheduled to be executed by 14 June 2027. 

• 🤝 The company confirmed no promoter interest and no related-party transaction in the contract. 

• 📅 RailTel received the work order on 15 June 2026 at 08:32 AM. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The order strengthens RailTel’s order book and reinforces its position in defense and telecom network infrastructure.)

reports.text:
NORTHERN ARC & YES BANK : STRATEGIC PARTNERSHIP TO BRIDGE INDIA'S CREDIT GAP 🤝💳

• 🏦 Northern Arc Capital has signed an MoU with YES BANK to accelerate credit deployment across underserved segments in India. 

• 🌐 The partnership will leverage Northern Arc’s network of 368 Originator Partners to facilitate credit growth for YES BANK. 

• ⚙️ Northern Arc’s proprietary platforms nPOS, NIMBUS and NuScore will be integrated with YES BANK’s digital lending architecture. 

• 💼 YES BANK will distribute Northern Arc’s wealth, bond and investment products, while NAIM will offer AIFs and PMS services to investors. 

• 📈 The alliance is expected to expand lending across MSME, rural and priority sectors, while creating new fee-based revenue streams. 

• 🤝 The collaboration stems from the shared strategic backing of SMBC, a key shareholder in both institutions. 

• 🚀 The partnership aims to drive financial inclusion, digital transformation and sustainable finance while enhancing customer acquisition and cross-selling opportunities. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The partnership strengthens Northern Arc’s funding ecosystem and provides YES BANK access to diversified credit opportunities, supporting long-term growth.)

reports.text:
Fredun Pharmaceuticals Limited | Q4 FY26 Key Concall Highlights

Key Business Updates
* Entered Hormone and Anti-Aging segments with dedicated product platforms
* Entered the anti-aging and wellness segment with a new range of NAD and NAD+ based products
* Wagr pet care platform expected to be launched in July
* Additional manufacturing capacity expansion underway, including 12–13 new packing lines by September

Business Segment Updates
* Mobilitics brand scheduled for launch in July with strong pre-booking response from physiotherapists
* Pet care ecosystem spans pharmaceuticals, nutraceuticals, grooming, diagnostics and functional foods
* New-age businesses witnessed strong traction with FY26 revenues of ~₹43 Cr in Pet Care, ~₹30 Cr in Mobility, ~₹26 Cr in Nutraceuticals and ~₹20–22 Cr in Cosmetics

Operational Updates
* More than 1,000 product registrations remain in the pipeline
* More than 80% of products are manufactured in-house
* Positive operating cash flows and improvement in working capital metrics

Key Financial Highlights
* Q4 FY26 Total Income stood at ₹213 Cr, EBITDA at ₹29 Cr and PAT at ₹11 Cr, reflecting YoY growth of 27%, 67% and 56%, respectively
* FY26 Total Income stood at ₹639 Cr, EBITDA at ₹95 Cr and PAT at ₹33 Cr, reflecting YoY growth of 40%, 72% and 60%, respectively

Growth Outlook
* Management expects FY27 top-line growth of 25%–30%
* New-age businesses continue to grow faster than traditional pharma operations

DUCON INFRATECHNOLOGIES : BOARD APPROVES ₹25 CR RIGHTS ISSUE 💰📈

• 🏦 Ducon Infratechnologies' board has approved a Rights Issue of up to ₹25 crore to strengthen its capital base. 

• 💸 The company plans to use up to ₹9.52 crore for repayment/prepayment of unsecured loans from promoter and MD Arun Govil. 

• ⚙️ Around ₹7.36 crore of the proceeds will be used to fund working capital requirements. 

• 📊 The issue aims to strengthen the balance sheet, optimize capital structure and enhance financial flexibility. 

• 👥 Existing shareholders will get an opportunity to participate in the company's future growth through the rights issue. 

• 📅 Details such as issue price, entitlement ratio and record date will be announced later in the Letter of Offer. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (The rights issue is aimed at deleveraging, improving liquidity and strengthening the company’s financial position for future growth.)

HCLTECH : INVESTS ₹1,427 CR IN SARVAM AI TO BUILD INDIA’S AI ECOSYSTEM 🤖🇮🇳

• 💰 HCLTech will invest ₹1,427.25 crore ($150 million) in Sarvam AI, acquiring a 10.46% stake in the company. 

• 🧠 Sarvam AI is India’s full-stack sovereign AI company, building AI models across language, speech, vision and documents. 

• 🚀 The funding will support development of next-generation AI models for agentic AI, coding and cybersecurity use cases. 

• 🌍 HCLTech plans to develop industry-specific AI solutions and language models for its global clients. 

• 🏛️ The partnership aims to accelerate sovereign AI deployments for governments, regulated industries and enterprises. 

• 📈 Sarvam AI raised $234 million in the first close of its Series B round at a $1.5 billion valuation. 

• 🤝 Sarvam already serves major enterprises including SBI Life, LIC, IDFC, Tata Capital and CRED. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens HCLTech’s AI capabilities and positions it at the forefront of India’s sovereign AI ecosystem.)

ADANI ENTERPRISES : PARTNERS WITH JABIL FOR AI DATA CENTER PLATFORM 🤖🏗️

• 🤝 Adani Enterprises and Jabil plan a strategic alliance to build an AI and data center infrastructure manufacturing platform in India. 

• 🖥️ The platform targets GW-scale AI rack manufacturing, including servers, storage, networking systems and liquid-cooled AI racks. 

• ⚡ The alliance will also manufacture critical infrastructure such as PDUs, CDUs, transformers, switchgears and thermal systems. 

• 🌍 The companies are targeting a $3 trillion global AI infrastructure opportunity over the next seven years. 

• 🇮🇳 The initiative aligns with Adani Group’s plan to develop 5 GW of green-powered AI-ready data centers by 2035. 

• 💼 The partnership is expected to create thousands of skilled jobs and position India as a global AI hardware export hub. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Adani’s AI and data center ambitions while positioning India as a major AI infrastructure manufacturing hub.)

reports.text:
DEVYANI INTERNATIONAL : NSE & BSE CLEAR MERGER WITH SAPPHIRE FOODS 🍕🍗

• 🟢 Devyani International has received NSE's ‘No Objection’ and BSE's ‘No Adverse Observations’ for its proposed merger with Sapphire Foods India. 

• 📑 The scheme was earlier approved by Devyani’s board on January 1, 2026, and now moves to the next approval stages. 

• ⚖️ The merger remains subject to approvals from shareholders, creditors, regulators, CCI and NCLT. 

• 🔄 SEBI has directed both companies to disclose details on shareholding changes, valuation methodology, legal proceedings and merger impact to shareholders.  

• ⏳ The observation letters are valid for six months, within which the scheme must be filed with NCLT. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Receipt of exchange approvals is a key milestone that de-risks the merger process and moves the transaction closer to completion.)

SJS ENTERPRISES : SELLS BENGALURU PROPERTY FOR ₹58.5 CR 🏢💰

• 🏢 SJS Enterprises has sold its land and building in Bengaluru, comprising 72,560 sq. ft. built-up area and 38,625 sq. ft. of land. 

• 💵 The property has been sold for a consideration of ₹58.5 crore, with the sale deed executed on June 15, 2026. 

• ⚙️ The company clarified that no business operations were being carried out from the property and hence the sale will not impact operations. 

• 🤝 The buyer does not belong to the promoter/promoter group, and the transaction is not a related-party transaction. 

• 📋 The sale does not constitute disposal of a substantial undertaking under the Companies Act, 2013. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Asset monetization unlocks value and strengthens liquidity without affecting core business operations.)

reports.text:
Bright Outdoor Media Limited Board Approves Migration to BSE & NSE Main Boards

The Board of Directors of Bright Outdoor Media Limited, at its meeting held on June 12, 2026, has approved the proposal for migration of the Company's equity shares from the BSE SME Platform to the Main Board of BSE Limited and the proposed listing on the Main Board of the National Stock Exchange of India Limited (NSE), subject to necessary regulatory and statutory approvals.

Bright Outdoor Media Limited was listed on the BSE SME Platform on March 24, 2023, through a successful IPO at an issue price of ₹146 per share, aggregating to approximately ₹55.48 Cr. Since its listing, the Company has continued to strengthen its presence across outdoor advertising, DOOH, transit advertising, railway and airport media formats, with an established footprint across Mumbai and Navi Mumbai.

The proposed Main Board migration marks an important milestone in the Company’s capital market journey and is expected to provide access to a broader investor base while enhancing the visibility and liquidity of its equity shares.

APOLLO HOSPITALS PROVIDES CLARITY ON HEALTHCO DE-MERGER 🏥

• 🔄 Apollo Hospitals shared additional details on the proposed demerger and listing of Apollo Healthtech Ltd, highlighting governance and shareholder safeguards. 

• 👥 Apollo Healthtech’s board will have at least 50% independent directors, ensuring strong corporate governance. 

• 👩‍💼 Shobana Kamineni is proposed to be appointed as Executive Chairperson, subject to regulatory and shareholder approvals. 

• 📉 Investor and promoter board nomination rights will lapse if their stake falls below 10%, with Articles to be amended accordingly.

• 🗳️ The Upside Agreement will require approval from public shareholders both before and after listing of Apollo Healthtech. 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Enhanced governance safeguards and clarity around the demerger strengthen investor confidence in Apollo Healthtech’s future listing.)

- Individual business up 11% YoY
- YTD growth reached 28%
- Protection demand continues improving

**Total Industry Growth**
- Total sum assured up 43%
- Strong growth during May
- YTD growth reached 61%
- Industry momentum remains healthy

**Protection Mix Improving**
- Sum assured outpaced APE growth
- Higher protection penetration visible
- Product mix improving steadily
- Positive long-term sector indicator

**SBI Life Performance**
- Individual sum assured up 37%
- Strong May performance reported
- YTD growth reached 48%
- Outperformed industry growth

**HDFC Life Performance**
- Individual sum assured up 20%
- Growth remained healthy in May
- Total sum assured flat
- Protection demand remains stable

**ICICI Prudential Life**
- Individual sum assured up 35%
- Strong protection business growth
- Total sum assured up 70%
- Among strongest performers

**Key Takeaway**
- Protection products driving growth
- Industry demand remains robust
- SBI Life and ICICI standout
VEDANTA DEMERGER Valuation per share by Brokerage

CLSA on Cement sector

Post correction over past three months, Indian cement stocks are now trading at c. 1SD below the historical median and implying no profitability improvement even in FY28 (vs exit FY26). 

While believe FY27 is likely to be soft due to cost pressures and muted demand growth, as war risk subsides, expect profitability to jump in FY28

See value across most stocks in sector but prefer Ultratech, Dalmia and Shree given a better growth outlook.

MORGAN STANLEY ON RELIANCE INDUSTRIES

Maintain Overweight

Analyst Top Pick

Maintain target price 1,803

Energy security policies and tighter refining markets seen keeping product spreads structurally stronger for longer

Oil to chemicals earnings supported despite higher logistics costs; company well placed on heavy and sour crude processing and diversified sourcing

Chemical cycle recovery aided by advantaged feedstocks through US ethane and captive naphtha expected to lift earnings 6-8% this year

Monetisation 4.0 underway as solar module, cell and restorage manufacturing ramps up; not fully reflected in

Al monetisation and data centre investments remain a show-me story for investors

Stock trading at 1.1x EV to invested capital, a 68% discount to domestic peers across verticals, similar to 2018 levels before significant outperformance

MS on Meesho

Maintain Equal-weight with TP of Rs 190

Acquiring Kirana Club to expand into B2B commerce play
Move should help it tap into a large market, extending its proven consumer marketplace playbook to the B2B side and boosting its B2C on the grocery side

Given the small deal size and the target's early-stage revenue profile, near-term P&L impact on Meesho should be negligible

Bernstein on Aurobindo Pharma

Maintain Market-perform with TP of Rs 1498

Aurobindo OAI: Quality execution falls short; near term pain, future capabilities strengthening
OAI does not by itself stop shipments

Estimate a base case of continued partial supply plus a freeze on the ~35 ANDAs pending from the site, as ~3–3.5% FY27 EPS drag
The site has history of OAI followed by warning letter in 2024

Believe this is yet another evidence of poor quality execution by Aurobindo

Nomura on Adani Ports

Maintain Buy; Hike TP to Rs 2080 from Rs 1850

Remains well-poised to capitalise on favourable industry tailwinds

Management expects healthy revenue/EBITDA CAGRs of 19%/18% over FY26-31

FY26 annual report analysis – Smooth sailing

Raise FY27-28F EBITDA by 1-2%

well-positioned to benefit from industry growth tailwinds.

@Stockupdate9

UBS on Suzlon

Buy, TP Rs 72

Investor day takeaways,

Management highlighted a clear strategic pivot to an integrated renewable energy (RE) platform targeting installed base share of 40% in five years in wind (vs 33% currently)

As compared to solar manufacturing peers, Suzlon’s integrated RE strategy is relatively asset-light whereby it intends to leverage on upcoming robust integrated solar manufacturing capacities via strategic tie ups

EPC & DevCo model will ensure reduced risk and better predictability/control in projects while delivering wind farms to customers.

Stock Fundamental in detail
@Fundamental3

Nomura on DR Reddys

Buy, TP Rs 1600

Announced launch of gBosulif (Bosutinib tabs) 400 mg in US, with 180- days exclusivity

Product has annual sales of USD214mn at company level

As per IQVIA, product experienced volume growth of 6% y-y in MAT April 2026.

DRRD has launched the product in partnership with MSN (unlisted), which is sole first ANDA filer for product

Launch is a positive surprise, as partnership with MSN was not disclosed earlier.

Assuming 30-40% price erosion during the exclusivity period and a 40% market share, expect product to contribute $25-30mn in revenue in FY26F

Stock Fundamental in detail
@Fundamental3
Mirae Asset Sharekhan Morning Tiger -29.pdf

Nomura on HDFC BK

Buy, TP Rs 950

Market concerns that have weighed on HDFCB are not independent problems.

Deposit growth lagging expectations, CD ratio above management's guidance, LCR lowest among large private peers, & a higher reliance on short-term wholesale funding

They are same problems viewed from different perspectives

RBI's FCNR (B) scheme could address all these in a single window, making HDFCB a potential standout beneficiary

Bank could garner 15% of overall FCNR flows, – equivalent to 3% of its current deposit base

Stock Fundamental in detail
@Fundamental3

Jefferies on Telecom

During FY26, sector revenues rose 10% YoY to a record US$32bn, led by ARPU growth and strong momentum in B- and C-Circles.

Bharti Airtel led sector gains, adding revenue market share across most circles, supported by premiumization and rural expansion.

VIL’s revenue rose 5% YoY, but subscriber losses drove further share erosion.

Over FY26-28E, expect revenues to grow at 13% CAGR to US$41bn, assuming a 15% tariff hike in Dec-26.

Stock Fundamental in detail
@Fundamental3
